candidate_llm_application: Wax-schedule fine-tuning: ordered monotonically-growing mask stack across N sequential dye stages

results.judge_justification: FIT's targeted layer attribution + redundancy filtering across a stream of unlearning operations is functionally a monotonically protected coordinate set across sequential stages — the wax-stack discipline.
Mechanistic analysis of forgetting; diagnostic only, not a wax-stack mitigation.

Low-perplexity token-mask explicitly masks tokens on which gradient is suppressed during fine-tune to preserve prior capabilities — operationally the per-stage wax mask on protected tokens. Repeating across stages would yield the candidate's monotonically growing stack.

Element-wise importance regularization with layer-wise coefficients across fine-tune — functionally a soft wax stack that protects high-importance coordinates from update. Repeated stage applications produce the candidate's monotonic protection.
Model growth approach; adds capacity instead of waxing prior coords.
